title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: rkagi R-first client Kagi APIs designed reproducible data workflows. package optimized users want : Build structured API requests R scripts. Run single batch requests uniform way. Persist raw API responses JSON traceability. Convert JSON outputs parquet analytics pipelines. document intended AI assistants (example Codex, Claude Code, similar tools) changes stay aligned project intent.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Query-first architecture. Users construct explicit query objects first, execute . Query construction execution separate concerns. Endpoint-specific constructors, shared execution engine. Constructors encode endpoint semantics; kagi_request() executes supported query objects. Reproducibility implicit behavior. Requests write JSON files disk. File-based outputs treated source--truth artifacts. Predictable scaling single batch. Constructors return named lists consistently one-query many-query workflows use calling pattern. Pragmatic fault tolerance. error_mode = "stop" strict runs; error_mode = "write_dummy" long pipelines partial completion preferred. Analysis handoff first-class workflow. kagi_request_parquet() converts JSON collections parquet downstream processing.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Expected user flow: Create connection kagi_connection(). Build query objects using endpoint-specific constructors. Execute kagi_request() output directory. Optionally convert kagi_request_parquet(). contract remain stable across releases.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Current endpoint constructors: search_query() enrich_web_query() enrich_news_query() summarize_query() fastgpt_query() constructors : Return named lists. Preserve endpoint-specific parameters without hidden coercion. accepted directly kagi_request().

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: kagi_request() supports two modes: error_mode = "stop": Fail fast raise error. error_mode = "write_dummy": Emit warning write structured dummy JSON payload. Dummy payload requirements: Include metadata failed request. Keep endpoint shape compatible possible. Use empty/null data fields missing payloads. Remain convertible kagi_request_parquet().

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: JSON files audit artifacts. Parquet derivative format analysis. Design implications: Never require parquet conversion core package usage. Prefer non-lossy conversion possible. Handle partial failures dummy payloads without breaking conversion.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Testing cassette-driven vcr ensure deterministic API behavior CI local re-runs. Priorities: Cover exported constructors request paths. Include failure-path tests, especially dummy-write mode. Validate parquet conversion mixed success/error outputs. Keep fixture layout consistent (tests/testthat/fixtures/cassettes).

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Documentation must user-oriented narrative, just code snippets. Required doc layers: README onboarding package overview. Quickstart vignette first end--end run. Endpoint vignettes deep usage patterns. Changelog-style document release deltas. Narrative guidelines: Explain showing code. Keep examples realistic pipeline-oriented. Include error-handling production guidance.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Agent-oriented operational guidance packaged inst/skills. maintainer-workflow covers implementation conventions, tests/cassettes, naming, release hygiene. Endpoint user skills (user-search, user-enrich, user-summarize, user-fastgpt) mirror endpoint vignettes. Skills intended strict execution guidance coding agents must remain aligned package behavior vignette examples.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Key project-level changes reflected cycle: Standardized query constructor behavior named lists. Added/expanded FastGPT endpoint support via fastgpt_query(). Added graceful fallback behavior kagi_request() dummy outputs. Ensured dummy outputs can flow parquet conversion. Strengthened tests around mixed success/failure request lists. Consolidated cassette location vcr helper setup. Updated quickstart added endpoint-focused vignettes. Aligned pkgdown output article organization. Added AI-agent skills inst/skills maintainer endpoint-specific workflows. Added standard disclaimer AI-assisted development notice README. Removed unused legacy assets inst/ (old extdata, plantuml diagrams, query_test script).

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: modifying repository, AI agents : Preserve query-constructor -> request-execution separation. Avoid introducing endpoint-specific logic generic file/IO paths unless required. Keep strict graceful error behavior tested. Prefer additive changes query schema; flag breaking parameter renames explicitly. Update tests, vignettes, changelog change set. Treat JSON output compatibility stability concern.

title: rkagi Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Potential next items evaluate: Additional Kagi endpoints beyond current coverage. explicit schema versioning dummy payloads. Optional validation helpers API key / endpoint health checks. Tighter invariants parquet schema across endpoint versions.

what: Enrich Endpoint: Context Discovery for Web and News
title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: Enrich API useful plain search result list enough want curated context topic. rkagi, split two constructors: enrich_web_query() web context. enrich_news_query() news context. execution flow identical . Assume tracking biodiversity policy institutional sources. constructors return named lists, single batch execution share interface. stage two independent JSON collections, one web context one news context. recurring monitoring, prepare topic vector run batch. pattern well suited weekly monthly trend snapshots. want long jobs continue one request fails: Failed requests written dummy JSON data = null plus error block, function emits warnings. Use parquet want join enrich data tables build reproducible reports. Keep web news outputs separate directories. Start workers = 1 debugging, increase. Keep raw JSON source truth, regenerate parquet needed.
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q_web <- enrich_web_query(   query = "open data portals",   site = "gov",   expand = FALSE )  q_news <- enrich_news_query(   query = "biodiversity policy",   expand = FALSE ) out_web <- "enrich_web" dir.create(out_web, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_web[[1]],   output = out_web,   overwrite = TRUE ) out_news <- "enrich_news" dir.create(out_news, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news[[1]],   output = out_news,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_news_batch <- enrich_news_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem restoration", "nature finance"),   expand = TRUE )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news_batch,   output = "enrich_news_batch",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2 ) kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news_batch,   output = "enrich_news_batch_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = "enrich_news_batch",   output = "enrich_news_batch_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

what: Build web and news enrich queries
title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: Assume tracking biodiversity policy institutional sources. constructors return named lists, single batch execution share interface.
code: q_web <- enrich_web_query(   query = "open data portals",   site = "gov",   expand = FALSE )  q_news <- enrich_news_query(   query = "biodiversity policy",   expand = FALSE )

what: Execute a focused run for each enrich type
title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: stage two independent JSON collections, one web context one news context.
code: out_web <- "enrich_web" dir.create(out_web, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_web[[1]],   output = out_web,   overwrite = TRUE ) out_news <- "enrich_news" dir.create(out_news, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news[[1]],   output = out_news,   overwrite = TRUE )

what: Move to a thematic batch workload
title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: recurring monitoring, prepare topic vector run batch. pattern well suited weekly monthly trend snapshots.
code: q_news_batch <- enrich_news_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem restoration", "nature finance"),   expand = TRUE )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news_batch,   output = "enrich_news_batch",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2 )

what: Handle request failures without losing the whole run
title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: want long jobs continue one request fails: Failed requests written dummy JSON data = null plus error block, function emits warnings.
code: kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news_batch,   output = "enrich_news_batch_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" )

title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: Use parquet want join enrich data tables build reproducible reports.
code: kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = "enrich_news_batch",   output = "enrich_news_batch_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: rkagi Enrich Endpoint Guide
text: Keep web news outputs separate directories. Start workers = 1 debugging, increase. Keep raw JSON source truth, regenerate parquet needed.

title: rkagi FastGPT Endpoint Guide
text: FastGPT endpoint use need direct prompt-response interactions optional web grounding. guide focuses structure prompts repeatable runs, scale batches, handle transient failures without dropping full job. Like query builders rkagi, returns named list consistency. Persisting responses JSON useful prompt comparison reproducibility. pattern suitable generating comparable outputs across fixed prompt set. request fails, dummy JSON written job continues. FastGPT, dummy records include data$output = null data$tokens = 0. gives clean table-like format reporting joins run metadata. Keep prompts concise task-specific. Version prompt sets explicitly scripts. Use graceful mode unattended batch processing.
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q_fast <- fastgpt_query(   query = "What is Python 3.11?",   cache = TRUE,   web_search = TRUE )  q_fast out_fast <- "fastgpt_output" dir.create(out_fast, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast[[1]],   output = out_fast,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_fast_many <- fastgpt_query(   query = c(     "What are ecosystem services?",     "What is biodiversity offsetting?",     "How does pollination support food systems?"   ),   cache = TRUE,   web_search = TRUE )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast_many,   output = "fastgpt_batch",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2 ) kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast_many,   output = "fastgpt_batch_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = "fastgpt_batch_safe",   output = "fastgpt_batch_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

what: Create one connection for all runs
title: rkagi FastGPT Endpoint Guide
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

text: Like query builders rkagi, returns named list consistency.
code: q_fast <- fastgpt_query(   query = "What is Python 3.11?",   cache = TRUE,   web_search = TRUE )  q_fast

what: Execute and store the first response

text: Persisting responses JSON useful prompt comparison reproducibility.
code: out_fast <- "fastgpt_output" dir.create(out_fast, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast[[1]],   output = out_fast,   overwrite = TRUE )

what: Scale prompts to a batch job

what: Keep long runs resilient with graceful error handling

code: kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast_many,   output = "fastgpt_batch_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" )

text: gives clean table-like format reporting joins run metadata.

text: Keep prompts concise task-specific. Version prompt sets explicitly scripts. Use graceful mode unattended batch processing.

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: quickstart complete first run rkagi: install package, create secure connection, build queries, execute requests, store responses format ready analysis. package intentionally structured around stable pattern: Create query objects endpoint-specific constructors. Reuse single kagi_connection() object. Execute kagi_request() persist JSON responses. Optionally convert JSON output parquet tables. pattern familiar, every endpoint follows operational logic. rkagi installed yet, install GitHub load : Store API key local keyring : create reusable connection object: Using function api_key keeps credentials scripts supports reproducible batch runs. Search good first endpoint shows query constructors work: search_query() returns named list, even single input. consistency makes easy scale code one query many. want inspect generated search string interactively: Create output folder run request: request writes JSON file. makes reruns audits straightforward. remaining endpoints use connection request function. query constructor changes. move inspection analysis pipelines, parquet usually convenient: deeper endpoint-specific workflows (batching patterns, robust error handling, endpoint-focused examples), continue : vignette("search-endpoint", package = "rkagi") vignette("enrich-endpoint", package = "rkagi") vignette("summarize-endpoint", package = "rkagi") vignette("fastgpt-endpoint", package = "rkagi")
code: if (requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = FALSE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("rkrug/rkagi") library(rkagi) keyring::key_set("API_kagi") conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q <- search_query(   query = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   inurl = c("2024", "report"),   intitle = "summary",   expand = FALSE ) open_search_query(q[[1]]) out_search <- tempfile("rkagi-search-") dir.create(out_search, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q,   limit = 5,   output = out_search,   overwrite = TRUE )  list.files(out_search, pattern = "\\.json$", full.names = TRUE) q_web <- enrich_web_query("open data portals", site = "gov", expand = FALSE)  out_web <- tempfile("rkagi-enrich-web-") dir.create(out_web, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_web,   output = out_web,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_news <- enrich_news_query("biodiversity policy", expand = FALSE)  out_news <- tempfile("rkagi-enrich-news-") dir.create(out_news, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_news,   output = out_news,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_sum_text <- summarize_query(   text = paste(     "Biodiversity underpins ecosystem services including pollination,",     "soil fertility, water purification, and climate regulation.",     "Species decline has implications for resilience and wellbeing."   ),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE )  out_sum <- tempfile("rkagi-summarize-") dir.create(out_sum, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_sum_text,   output = out_sum,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_fast <- fastgpt_query(   query = "What are ecosystem services?",   cache = TRUE,   web_search = TRUE )  out_fast <- tempfile("rkagi-fastgpt-") dir.create(out_fast, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_fast,   output = out_fast,   overwrite = TRUE ) parquet_dir <- tempfile("rkagi-parquet-")  kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = out_search,   output = parquet_dir,   overwrite = TRUE ) sessionInfo()

what: Search Endpoint: From Question to Reusable Search Pipeline
title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: Search endpoint usually first place users build production workflows rkagi. guide follows one realistic path: starting single question, refining query syntax, scaling batches, choosing error strategy matches use case. create reuse every request script project. Suppose collecting policy reports related biodiversity. want PDFs DOCX files, hosted specific sites, year hints URL. q named list. Even single query, useful downstream code works one query one hundred. want validate built, open directly browser: point stable JSON output can inspected, versioned, reprocessed. monitor multiple themes sources, use expand = TRUE generate combinations. Run batch: pattern appropriate recurring jobs weekly monitoring. interactive work CI failures stop execution, use strict mode: long-running collection pipelines partial progress better full abort, use graceful mode: graceful mode, failed requests write dummy JSON records data = null plus error metadata, warning issued. collection complete, convert JSON folder parquet: Parquet output easier query downstream analytics pipelines. Keep query construction execution separate script sections. Use meaningful output folder names tied run date topic. Use error_mode = "stop" QA/CI "write_dummy" large unattended runs.
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q <- search_query(   query = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   inurl = c("2024", "report"),   intitle = "summary",   expand = FALSE ) open_search_query(q[[1]]) out_single <- "search_single" dir.create(out_single, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q[[1]],   limit = 5,   output = out_single,   overwrite = TRUE )  list.files(out_single, full.names = TRUE) q_many <- search_query(   query = c("biodiversity indicators", "ecosystem services"),   site = c("ipbes.net", "cbd.int"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   expand = TRUE )  length(q_many) out_batch <- "search_batch" dir.create(out_batch, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_many,   limit = 3,   output = out_batch,   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2 ) kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q[[1]],   limit = 1,   output = "search_strict",   overwrite = TRUE,   error_mode = "stop" ) kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_many,   limit = 1,   output = "search_graceful",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = out_batch,   output = "search_batch_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: create reuse every request script project.
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

code: out_single <- "search_single" dir.create(out_single, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q[[1]],   limit = 5,   output = out_single,   overwrite = TRUE )  list.files(out_single, full.names = TRUE)

what: Scale from single query to query grid
title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: monitor multiple themes sources, use expand = TRUE generate combinations. Run batch: pattern appropriate recurring jobs weekly monitoring.
code: q_many <- search_query(   query = c("biodiversity indicators", "ecosystem services"),   site = c("ipbes.net", "cbd.int"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   expand = TRUE )  length(q_many) out_batch <- "search_batch" dir.create(out_batch, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_many,   limit = 3,   output = out_batch,   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2 )

title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: interactive work CI failures stop execution, use strict mode: long-running collection pipelines partial progress better full abort, use graceful mode: graceful mode, failed requests write dummy JSON records data = null plus error metadata, warning issued.
code: kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q[[1]],   limit = 1,   output = "search_strict",   overwrite = TRUE,   error_mode = "stop" ) kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_many,   limit = 1,   output = "search_graceful",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 2,   error_mode = "write_dummy" )

what: Convert search JSON to parquet for analysis
title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: collection complete, convert JSON folder parquet: Parquet output easier query downstream analytics pipelines.
code: kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = out_batch,   output = "search_batch_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: rkagi Search Endpoint Guide
text: Keep query construction execution separate script sections. Use meaningful output folder names tied run date topic. Use error_mode = "stop" QA/CI "write_dummy" large unattended runs.

what: Summarize Endpoint: Robust Summaries in Real Pipelines
title: rkagi Summarize Endpoint Guide
text: Universal Summarizer endpoint often users need quality resilience. practice, summarization workloads mixed: inputs ideal, short, fail upstream reasons outside script. guide shows keep pipelines reliable preserving useful output. rkagi supports URL-based raw-text summarization. calls return named lists, execution stays consistent regardless input style. baseline path standard inputs. short text can trigger server-side failures (example minimum document size constraints). want full pipeline stop, use graceful mode. mode, request warns writes dummy JSON payload, summarize fields present empty (data$output = null, data$tokens = 0). realistic batch often contains valid invalid inputs. keeps successful summary recording structured placeholder failed item. Parquet conversion allows downstream analysis retaining run-level consistency. Use meaningful minimum text lengths sending summarize requests. Use workers = 1 diagnosing warnings, scale . Prefer error_mode = "write_dummy" long unattended jobs.
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q_url <- summarize_query(   url = "https://www.example.com/long-article",   engine = "muriel",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE ) q_text <- summarize_query(   text = paste(     "Biodiversity underpins ecosystem services such as pollination, soil fertility,",     "water purification, and climate regulation.",     "Habitat loss and climate pressure accelerate species decline with consequences",     "for resilience and human wellbeing."   ),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "takeaway",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE ) out_sum <- "summarize_output" dir.create(out_sum, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_text[[1]],   output = out_sum,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_short <- summarize_query(   text = "Too short.",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_short[[1]],   output = "summarize_short_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) q_ok <- summarize_query(   text = paste(rep("Long summarize input text.", 40), collapse = " "),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  q_err <- summarize_query(   text = "short",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = list(ok = q_ok[[1]], err = q_err[[1]]),   output = "summarize_mixed",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 1,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = "summarize_mixed",   output = "summarize_mixed_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: rkagi Summarize Endpoint Guide
code: library(rkagi)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

what: Build summarize queries for URL and text sources

code: q_url <- summarize_query(   url = "https://www.example.com/long-article",   engine = "muriel",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE ) q_text <- summarize_query(   text = paste(     "Biodiversity underpins ecosystem services such as pollination, soil fertility,",     "water purification, and climate regulation.",     "Habitat loss and climate pressure accelerate species decline with consequences",     "for resilience and human wellbeing."   ),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "takeaway",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE )

title: rkagi Summarize Endpoint Guide
text: short text can trigger server-side failures (example minimum document size constraints). want full pipeline stop, use graceful mode. mode, request warns writes dummy JSON payload, summarize fields present empty (data$output = null, data$tokens = 0).
code: q_short <- summarize_query(   text = "Too short.",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_short[[1]],   output = "summarize_short_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   error_mode = "write_dummy" )

code: q_ok <- summarize_query(   text = paste(rep("Long summarize input text.", 40), collapse = " "),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  q_err <- summarize_query(   text = "short",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = list(ok = q_ok[[1]], err = q_err[[1]]),   output = "summarize_mixed",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 1,   error_mode = "write_dummy" )

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: R client Kagi API.

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: rkagi provides lightweight R interface Kagi API, including: Search API — perform web searches advanced operators Enrich API — get higher-signal results specialized indices Universal Summarizer — summarize text URLs one call FastGPT API — ask grounded LLM questions optional web context package follows rOpenSci style API clients: S3 classes connections, requests, results Extractor helpers work results tibbles text Secure API key handling keyring

title: Kagi API Client for R
code: # Install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rkrug/rkagi")

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: need Kagi account API access (paid plan). Store API key securely system keychain: package resolve key request time :
code: # Run once to save your key in the keychain keyring::key_set("API_kagi") conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key  = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

title: Kagi API Client for R
code: library(rkagi)  # Build a query q <- search_query(   query    = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = "pdf",   site     = "example.com",   expand   = FALSE )  # Execute request and write JSON output conn <- kagi_connection(api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi")) out <- tempfile("rkagi-search-") dir.create(out, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q,   limit = 3,   output = out,   overwrite = TRUE )

what: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
text: Construct one query strings Kagi Search API combining free-text terms structured operators filetype:, site:, inurl:, intitle:. Use kagi_request() execute request obtain json replies.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
code: enrich_news_query(   query,   filetype = NULL,   site = NULL,   inurl = NULL,   intitle = NULL,   expand = TRUE )

title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
text: query Character vector free-text query terms (required). can include quoted phrases boolean operators. filetype Optional character vector file type extensions (e.g. "pdf", "docx"). prefixed filetype:. site Optional character vector domains (e.g. "example.com", "gov"). prefixed site:. inurl Optional character vector URL substrings must present result URL. prefixed inurl:. intitle Optional character vector terms must appear page title. prefixed intitle:. expand Logical, default TRUE. TRUE, generate fully crossed set queries (Cartesian product combinations query, filetype, site, inurl, intitle). FALSE, concatenate arguments single combined query string.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
text: named list containing query strings class kagi_enrich_news_query, used kagi_request().

title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
text: helper makes easy build reproducible, complex queries structured operators. Use expand = TRUE want possible combinations (useful systematic search contexts). Use expand = FALSE want single combined query.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — enrich_news_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single combined query search_query(   query = "biodiversity",   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = "example.com",   expand = FALSE )  # Expanded combinations search_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   expand = TRUE )  # Open a generated query manually in browser open_search_query(search_query("openalex api", site = "docs.openalex.org")[[1]]) } # }

code: enrich_web_query(   query,   filetype = NULL,   site = NULL,   inurl = NULL,   intitle = NULL,   expand = TRUE )

TRUE, generate fully crossed set queries (Cartesian product combinations query, filetype, site, inurl, intitle). FALSE, concatenate arguments single combined query string.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single combined query search_query(   query = "biodiversity",   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = "example.com",   expand = FALSE )  # Expanded combinations search_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   expand = TRUE )  # Open a generated query manually in browser open_search_query(search_query("openalex api", site = "docs.openalex.org")[[1]]) } # }

what: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
text: Construct one FastGPT query payloads POST /fastgpt. Use kagi_request() execute request obtain JSON responses.

title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
code: fastgpt_query(query, cache = TRUE, web_search = TRUE)

title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
text: query Character vector. Query text answer. cache Logical. Whether cached responses allowed. Default: TRUE. web_search Logical. Whether use web search enrichment. Default: TRUE.

title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
text: named list query objects class kagi_fastgpt_query used kagi_request().

title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
text: According current Kagi FastGPT API behavior, web_search = FALSE service rejected. constructor enforces web_search = TRUE.

title: Create a FastGPT query payload — fastgpt_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fastgpt_query("Python 3.11") fastgpt_query(c("Python 3.11", "What is biodiversity?")) } # }

what: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
title: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
text: Build typed S3 object class kagi_connection holds basic configuration required talk Kagi API. includes API base URL, authentication key, retry settings.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
code: kagi_connection(   base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",   api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),   max_tries = 3 )

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
text: base_url Character scalar. Base URL Kagi API. Defaults "https://kagi.com/api/v0". api_key API key used authentication. default read environment variable KAGI_API_KEY. Best practice set variable ~/.Renviron. Advanced users may also supply function resolves key lazily request time (see resolve_api_key()). max_tries Integer scalar. Maximum number retry attempts transient errors. Defaults 3.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
text: object class kagi_connection components: base_url Base API URL. api_key API key (function resolve ). max_tries Maximum retry attempts.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — kagi_connection
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic connection (API key from env var) conn <- kagi_connection() conn  # Explicit API key conn2 <- kagi_connection(api_key = "my-key")  # Lazy API key via keyring conn3 <- kagi_connection(api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi")) } # }

what: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
title: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
text: Execute one rkagi query objects Kagi API write raw JSON responses disk. function supports search, enrich (web/news), summarize, FastGPT query classes generated search_query(), enrich_web_query(), enrich_news_query(), summarize_query(), fastgpt_query().

title: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
code: kagi_request(   connection,   query,   limit = NULL,   output = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   append = FALSE,   workers = 1,   verbose = FALSE,   error_mode = c("stop", "write_dummy") )

title: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
text: connection kagi_connection() object. query query object class kagi_search_query, kagi_summarize_query, kagi_enrich_web_query, kagi_enrich_news_query, kagi_fastgpt_query, list objects. limit Optional integer limit used search enrich endpoints. output Directory JSON response files written. overwrite Logical. TRUE, existing output deleted writing. append Logical. TRUE, write existing output directory instead deleting . workers Number parallel workers use query list. verbose Logical indicating whether progress messages shown. error_mode Error handling mode. "stop" (default) throws request errors. "write_dummy" writes fallback JSON payload returns output.

title: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
text: normalized path output.

title: Execute Kagi API requests and save JSON responses — kagi_request
text: query list query objects, requests executed parallel (using workers) query written named subdirectory output. Files written {endpoint}_{page}.json (example search_1.json). Pagination handled via meta$next_cursor provided API.

what: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
title: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
text: Convert directory JSON files written kagi_request() Apache Parquet dataset. JSON files processed one--one written partitioned parquet page.

title: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
code: kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = NULL,   output = NULL,   add_columns = list(),   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   delete_input = FALSE )

title: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
text: input_json Directory containing JSON files kagi_request(). output output directory parquet dataset; default: temporary directory. add_columns List additional fields added output. provided named list, e.g. list(column_1 = "value_1", column_2 = 2). Scalar values supported. overwrite Logical indicating whether overwrite output. verbose Logical indicating whether print progress information. Defaults TRUE delete_input Determines input_json deleted afterwards. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
text: Returns output invisibly parquet files written; otherwise NULL.

title: Convert JSON files to Apache Parquet files — kagi_request_parquet
text: function uses DuckDB read JSON files create Apache Parquet files. creates -memory DuckDB connection, reads JSON response, writes endpoint-specific tabular data parquet dataset. Files data = null skipped.

title: Open a Kagi search in the browser — open_search_query
text: Open Kagi search browser

title: Open a Kagi search in the browser — open_search_query
code: open_search_query(query, session_token = NULL)

title: Open a Kagi search in the browser — open_search_query

what: Resolve API key from a connection object — resolve_api_key
title: Resolve API key from a connection object — resolve_api_key
text: Resolve API key connection object

title: Resolve API key from a connection object — resolve_api_key
code: resolve_api_key(api_key)

what: Build a User-Agent string for rkagi — rkagi_user_agent
title: Build a User-Agent string for rkagi — rkagi_user_agent
text: Build User-Agent string rkagi

title: Build a User-Agent string for rkagi — rkagi_user_agent
code: rkagi_user_agent()

what: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
text: Construct one query strings Kagi Search API combining free-text terms structured operators filetype:, site:, inurl:, intitle:. Queries can either concatenated single string expanded Cartesian product combinations.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
code: search_query(   query,   filetype = NULL,   site = NULL,   inurl = NULL,   intitle = NULL,   expand = TRUE,   open_in_browser = FALSE )

title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
text: query Character vector free-text query terms (required). can include quoted phrases boolean operators. filetype Optional character vector file type extensions (e.g. "pdf", "docx"). prefixed filetype:. site Optional character vector domains (e.g. "example.com", "gov"). prefixed site:. inurl Optional character vector URL substrings must present result URL. prefixed inurl:. intitle Optional character vector terms must appear page title. prefixed intitle:. expand Logical, default TRUE. TRUE, generate fully crossed set queries (Cartesian product combinations query, filetype, site, inurl, intitle). FALSE, concatenate arguments single combined query string. open_in_browser Logical, default FALSE. TRUE, generated query immediately opened default web browser via open_search_query() inspection.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
text: named list containing query strings type kagi_search_query, used kagi_request().

title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
text: helper makes easy build reproducible, complex queries structured operators. Use expand = TRUE want possible combinations (useful systematic search contexts). Use expand = FALSE want single combined query.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — search_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single combined query search_query(   query = "biodiversity",   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = "example.com",   expand = FALSE )  # Expanded combinations search_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   expand = TRUE )  # Immediately open in browser search_query("openalex api", site = "docs.openalex.org", open_in_browser = TRUE) } # }

what: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
text: Construct typed S3 object class kagi_summarize describes Universal Summarizer request. Use kagi_request() execute request obtain json replies.

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
code: summarize_query(   url = NULL,   text = NULL,   engine = NULL,   summary_type = NULL,   target_language = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
text: url Optional character scalar. URL summarized. Mutually exclusive text. text Optional character scalar. Raw text summarized. Mutually exclusive url. engine Character scalar. Summarizer engine (options: "cecil", "agnes", "muriel", "daphne"). Default: "cecil". summary_type Character scalar. Type summary requested (options: "summary", "takeaway"). Default: "summary". target_language Character scalar. Target language ISO code. Supported codes: "EN", "BG", "CS", "DA", "DE", "EL", "ES", "ET", "FI", "FR", "HU", "ID", "", "JA", "KO", "LT", "LV", "NB", "NL", "PL", "PT", "RO", "RU", "SK", "SL", "SV", "TR", "UK", "ZH", "ZH-HANT". Default: "EN". cache Logical. Whether allow API-side caching.

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
text: named list kagi_summarize_query objects passed kagi_request().

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — summarize_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ req <- summarize_query(text = "Lorem ipsum") req } # }

title: rkagi 0.3.0
text: Added endpoint-specific query support FastGPT via fastgpt_query(). Standardized query constructor outputs named lists across endpoints. Extended kagi_request() error_mode ("stop", "write_dummy") strict vs resilient execution modes. Improved handling mixed query lists long batch runs can continue structured dummy outputs failure. Kept JSON--parquet first-class path via kagi_request_parquet() downstream analytics. maintainer-workflow user-search, user-enrich, user-summarize, user-fastgpt

title: rkagi 0.3.0
text: Fixed inconsistent request handling single-query list-query execution paths. Fixed recursive/list request execution propagate request options (including error handling mode) correctly. Fixed output path behavior single-item list requests create unexpected nested output directories. Removed redundant API key resolution calls connection flow applicable. Fixed fixture-location inconsistencies tests using single cassette location.

title: rkagi 0.3.0
text: Query constructors now consistently return named lists. Code previously assumed bare single query object may require [[1]] indexing direct calls. Error behavior can now configured explicitly; strict failure remains default, resilient mode writes dummy payloads warnings instead stopping.

title: rkagi 0.3.0
text: Updated package docs align current function names design principles. Reworked kagi_request documentation remove outdated references clarify endpoint behavior. Quickstart guide Search endpoint guide Enrich endpoint guide Summarize endpoint guide FastGPT endpoint guide Shifted vignettes toward user-oriented narrative style retaining runnable code examples. Updated pkgdown article structure clearer endpoint-based navigation. Added project-level design/context documentation maintainers AI coding agents (PROJECT_DESIGN.md). Added README disclaimer AI-assisted development notice.

title: rkagi 0.3.0
text: Added CI workflow package checks pull requests main pushes dev. Removed unused legacy package assets inst/ (extdata, plantuml, query_test.R).
